items.desc: Sometimes we do things that are simple. Learn an 'easy' phrase to describe this.

items.title: As sick as a parrot
items.desc: Learn a phrase about disappointment.

items.title: Eke out
items.desc: We have the perfect phrase to describe making the most of what you’ve got left.

items.title: Say no more
items.desc: Learn a phrase about understanding someone.

items.title: Scrub up well
items.desc: Learn a phrase about making a special effort to dress smartly.

items.title: You shouldn't have
items.desc: Learn a phrase to show surprise when receiving a gift

items.title: Not looking too clever
items.desc: If someone’s 'not looking too clever', are we doubting their intelligence?

items.title: Not like the sound of something
items.desc: Don't like what you hear? Say it with this expression!

items.title: I'll give you that
items.desc: Learn what to say when you agree with someone about some or all of what they said.

items.title: Another string to your bow
items.desc: Learn a phrase about having an additional skill.

items.title: Clap back
items.desc: When someone criticises something you’ve done, what do you do?

items.title: Talk through your hat
items.desc: Learn a phrase about talking nonsense.

items.title: Quote ... unquote
items.desc: An expression for when we want to repeat exactly what someone has said.

items.title: Not a patch on something
items.desc: Learn an expression for when something is far worse than something else.

items.title: No cap
items.desc: Do you know someone who always tells the truth?

items.title: Moreish
items.desc: An expression you can use to talk food you can't resist.

items.title: A legend in one's lifetime
items.desc: Do you know a famous living person? Here’s a phrase to describe them.

items.title: Live rent-free in one's head
items.desc: What or who do you think about all the time?

items.title: Now you're talking
items.desc: Learn how to show that you like someone’s idea.

items.title: Hits different
items.desc: Here’s the expression you can use to talk about something special.

items.title: Extra
items.desc: Learn an expression to describe how exaggerated some people are.

items.title: Zhuzh
items.desc: Do you want to make something more interesting by changing it slightly?

items.title: Lo and behold
items.desc: Learn an expression to introduce a surprising part of a story.

items.title: Bigwig
items.desc: Here’s an expression to describe an important and powerful person.

items.title: Cringe
items.desc: A word for when you want to say that something is really embarrassing.

items.title: It’s the … for me
items.desc: Learn an expression to talk about why you love or hate something.

items.title: Obvs
items.desc: Some things are obvious but maybe this word isn’t. Listen to the programme.

items.title: Down / Up for something
items.desc: If there is something you are interested in doing now, we have an expression for you.

items.title: Savage
items.desc: Learn a new meaning of the word 'savage'.

items.title: Lit
items.desc: Learn an informal word to say something is amazing.

items.title: Yikes!
items.desc: Learn an informal word to express shock.

items.title: YOLO
items.desc: Do you live for the moment?

items.title: Vibe
items.desc: If you want to say something has a good aura, we have an expression you can use.

items.title: Noob
items.desc: Do you know what to call someone who’s new to something or very inexperienced?

items.title: Adulting
items.desc: Learn a word to describe behaving like an adult.

items.title: Push back
items.desc: Learn a phrase about delaying things.

items.title: The CEO of
items.desc: If you want to say you’re the best at something, you could use this expression!

items.title: Zip it!
items.desc: We all like to talk, but some people don’t know when to stop.

items.title: Botch job
items.desc: What do you call a job badly done?

items.title: It slaps!
items.desc: What do you think is absolutely amazing?

items.title: Have a lot of plates spinning
items.desc: A phrase about having a lot of things to do.

items.title: Pizzazz
items.desc: Do a lot of people remark on how glamorous you are?

items.title: Oomph
items.desc: A word to describe someone with charm and energy!

items.title: Snap up
items.desc: If you snap up something, you get it quickly or immediately and usually with enthusiasm.

items.title: Nothingburger
items.desc: Describing something insignificant

items.title: Awesomesauce
items.desc: Learn a new informal word to describe something amazing.

items.title: Clout
items.desc: Learn an expression about having influence.

items.title: GOAT
items.desc: 'GOAT' is used to talk about things or people that we think are the best.

items.title: Workation
items.desc: 'Workation' is a combination of the words 'work' and 'vacation'.

items.title: Moonshot
items.desc: Learn an expression used to describe an experimental project.

items.title: Slap-bang
items.desc: Learn a phrase to describe an exact position.

items.title: Sadfishing
items.desc: A word used when someone exaggerates their emotional problems to get attention.

items.title: Mic drop
items.desc: Roy and Feifei teach you a new English expression.

items.title: Tone deaf
items.desc: Learn when 'tone deaf' has nothing to do with music...

items.title: 'A' game
items.desc: Sometimes people need to be on top form and do their best work.
